options: Create a RACI matrix for a software migration project. Rows: requirements sign-off, vendor selection, data migration, UAT, go-live approval, and post-go-live support. Columns: Project Manager, IT Lead, Business Owner, Finance, and External Vendor. One R per row, enforce RACI rules.
Please make a RACI chart for our project that clearly shows everyone's responsibilities across all the activities and all the different people involved.
Build a RACI matrix.
hint: Listing specific activities and named roles ensures the RACI reflects the actual project structure, and the one-R rule keeps accountability clear.

title: Project charter one-pager prompt
originalPrompt: You are starting a new initiative to migrate your CRM system. You need a project charter to get sign-off.
options: Write a one-page project charter for a CRM migration project. Sections: project purpose (one sentence), objectives (3 SMART goals), scope (in-scope and out-of-scope list), key stakeholders, timeline (start–end dates), budget (£X), and success criteria. Approver signature block at the bottom.
Write a project charter for our CRM migration.

Create a comprehensive project charter document for our CRM migration that covers everything stakeholders need to know about the project including the full business case, detailed scope, governance structure, resource plan, budget, risk register, and success metrics.
hint: Fixing seven sections with defined content per section and a one-page constraint means the charter is readable by a busy exec and ready for sign-off.

title: Tight project closure report prompt
originalPrompt: You need a concise prompt to generate a project closure report in under 100 tokens.
options: Write a project closure report. Sections: objectives met (Y/N + one line per objective), budget outturn (planned vs actual with % variance), schedule outturn (planned vs actual end date), top 3 lessons learned, and formal closure recommendation. Max 300 words.
Write a project closure report.

Please write a comprehensive and detailed project closure report that covers all aspects of the project including a full review of objectives achievement, financial performance, schedule performance, lessons learned, team performance, stakeholder feedback, and recommendations for future similar projects.
hint: Five defined sections with content constraints per section and a 300-word cap produces a concise closure document that governance boards actually read.

title: Rewrite dependency map prompt
originalPrompt: Map out the dependencies in our programme. We have several projects that affect each other.
options: Create a programme dependency map for these 6 projects [list them]. For each dependency: Project A → Project B, dependency type (FS/SS/FF/SF), and impact if delayed (H/M/L). Output as a table. Highlight any circular dependencies.
Map all the dependencies between our programme's projects and show how they are connected and what the impacts of delays might be.
Create a comprehensive dependency mapping exercise for our programme that identifies all the inter-project dependencies and shows the critical path and potential bottlenecks.
hint: Using standard dependency types (FS/SS/FF/SF) and requesting a circular dependency check means the map supports scheduling decisions rather than just documenting connections.

title: Issue log template prompt
originalPrompt: Your team keeps raising issues informally. You need a structured issue log for the project.
options: Create an issue log template for a medium-complexity IT project. Columns: Issue ID, Date Raised, Description, Raised By, Severity (H/M/L), Assigned Owner, Target Resolution Date, Status (Open/In Progress/Closed), and Resolution Notes. Pre-populate with 2 example rows.
Build an issue log for our project.
Create a comprehensive issue log template that captures all the information we need to track and resolve project issues effectively and has all the fields needed for good project governance.
hint: Naming nine specific columns and requesting two pre-populated example rows means the template is ready to use and self-explanatory to new team members.

title: Rewrite programme governance framework
originalPrompt: Set up a governance framework for our programme. We want to make sure everything is overseen properly.
options: Design a programme governance framework for a £5M, 18-month transformation. Define: governance tiers (Programme Board, Steering Co, Workstream Leads), meeting cadence per tier, reporting pack contents per tier, decision rights matrix (who approves budget changes, scope changes, risks), and escalation path. One-page summary table.
Create a governance framework for our programme that makes sure everything is properly overseen and the right people are involved in decisions.
Write a comprehensive programme governance framework that covers all aspects of programme oversight including committee structures, reporting, decision-making, risk management, and change control.
hint: Naming three governance tiers, defining cadence, report contents, and decision rights per tier, and requesting a one-page summary table means the framework is implementable, not theoretical.

title: Concentration risk analysis prompt
originalPrompt: Your top 3 customers represent 65% of revenue. You need to assess and present concentration risk.
options: Analyse customer concentration risk for a business where top 3 clients = 65% of ARR. Cover: risk statement (what happens if one leaves), financial impact scenario (loss of largest client at £Xk ARR), early warning indicators, and 3 mitigation actions (diversification targets, contract terms, pipeline mix). Board-ready summary, max 1 page.
Help me explain our customer concentration risk to the board.

Write a comprehensive analysis of our customer concentration risk covering all aspects of the risk including the financial impact, strategic implications, mitigation options, and our plan to diversify our customer base over time.
hint: Providing the 65% concentration figure, structuring four elements, and specifying board audience with a page limit produces a self-contained risk briefing.

title: Rewrite insurance coverage review prompt
originalPrompt: Review our insurance coverage and let me know if we have the right policies in place.
options: Review our insurance portfolio against our business risk profile (200-person B2B SaaS, £15M revenue, 50+ enterprise contracts). For each policy type—Professional Indemnity, Cyber, D&O, Employers' Liability, Product Liability—state: coverage limit, typical adequacy for our profile, and coverage gap if any. Table format.
Tell me if our insurance policies are adequate for our business.

Review all our insurance policies and check whether the coverage levels are appropriate given the nature and scale of our business activities and identify any gaps or areas where we might be underinsured.
hint: Specifying company size and revenue, naming five policy types, and requiring three columns including a gap assessment produces a structured insurance review the CFO can act on.

title: Quick risk treatment options
originalPrompt: You need a tight prompt asking AI to recommend a risk treatment strategy for a specific risk.
options: For this risk: [paste risk description], recommend the optimal treatment strategy from: avoid, reduce, transfer, or accept. State the chosen strategy, rationale in one sentence, one action required, and estimated residual risk (H/M/L) after treatment.
What should we do about this risk?

Please analyse this risk in detail and recommend whether we should avoid it, reduce it, transfer it, or accept it, providing a full explanation of each option and why you have chosen the recommended approach.
hint: Constraining the response to four named strategies, one-sentence rationale, one action, and a residual risk rating produces a decision-ready recommendation in minimal tokens.

title: Vendor single source risk prompt
originalPrompt: You rely on a single vendor for a critical software component. You need to assess and present this dependency risk.
options: Assess single-vendor dependency risk for our reliance on [Vendor X] for our core payment processing API. Cover: risk if vendor fails or exits, business impact (revenue at risk £X), current mitigations (contractual protections, escrow), and recommended actions (alternative vendor qualification, redundancy build). Practical, concise.
Help me understand the risk of relying on just one vendor for our software.

Write a comprehensive vendor dependency risk assessment that covers all aspects of our reliance on this single vendor including the technical, financial, operational, and strategic risks and all the mitigation options available to us.
hint: Naming the vendor, specifying the revenue-at-risk figure, and structuring four elements (risk, impact, mitigations, actions) produces a usable one-page risk briefing.
